user_submissions.submission:
jerrythea commented on Medieval Madness (Remake Royal Edition) (Chicago Gaming, 2015) at Glimmerhold (Tabletop Tycoon) in Manchester. They said: The staff responded to my E-Mail the next day with a detailed explanation:

Regarding Medieval Madness, the slope was set a little steep by the previous owner, so it did play pretty fast. 

While the "standard" was set at 6.5 by the manufacturer, some games play better with some adjustments, and MM is one of those titles a lot of people seem to like steeper. 

I gave it a quick play, and I could feel it was a bit fast though, especially for general public use. 

I've reduced the slope a bit, so it's probably around 7.0 or so now (it was over 8 previously). 

Gameplay is a little slower now, but you'll probably find it more comfortable and predictable. 

Hopefully that helps! 

Appreciate the feedback - sadly I never have time to play the games much, so noticing things like this often takes some customer feedback. 

Thanks!

Appreciate the feedback - sadly I never have time to play the games much, so noticing things like this often takes some customer feedback. 

Thanks!

user_submissions.submission:
jerrythea commented on Medieval Madness (Remake Royal Edition) (Chicago Gaming, 2015) at Glimmerhold (Tabletop Tycoon) in Manchester. They said: I am a bit of a medieval madness!expert, and it looks like this machine, although in pristine condition, is not playing correctly.

I am not sure if it is the leveling of the machine, or if the flippers are placed wrong, or need to be replaced.

Examples of this are, on the first skill shot, when, holding the left flipper and then releasing, the ball will simply drain down to the middle, instead of bouncing off the left flipper. 

A more significant flaw I have seen with this machine, when the ball is kicked out of the right saucer, it does not come down the playfield correctly, and without nudging the machine, the ball will drain down the middle. 

I did notice there is a leveling bubble vial, on the bottom, right, and all that is visible is the yellow fluid.

I am pretty sure that there should be a bubble in the middle of the vial.
